channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian annual inflation climbs to 4.6% amid global fuel supply disruptions
item.description: In March, Australia's annual inflation rate surged to 4.6 per cent, marking the fastest price growth in over two years. The spike is largely attributed to a disruption in global oil supplies caused by...

item.title: FIA to occupy Cotton Exchange building as Karachi HQ after KCA eviction
item.description: KARACHI: The Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) is moving to establish its Karachi headquarters in the historic Cotton Exchange building on I.I. Chundrigar Road. Earlier, in December 2025, the FIA alo...

item.title: Amber Ray celebrates Kennedy Rapudo’s 40th birthday with family and friends
item.description: Kenyan socialite Amber Ray went all out to make her partner Kennedy Rapudo’s 40th birthday a memorable one. She organised a classy black-elegance themed party and invited close family and friends, kee...

item.title: ICE Arrests Illegal Immigrant in Fatal Oregon Crash That Killed Newlyweds
item.description: Rajinder Kumar, a 32-year-old Indian national, has been arrested by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) after being released from an Oregon jail. Authorities say Kumar caused a deadly crash...

item.title: China’s Approach to Rural Development Offers Lessons for Global Poverty Reduction
item.description: For decades, development thinking often assumed that progress required urbanisation at the expense of rural areas. China is challenging this notion. After declaring a victory over absolute poverty ear...

item.title: Kaduna State Reviews Environmental Policy to Tackle Pollution and Climate Challenges
item.description: Kaduna State Government don start to review the 2019 Environmental Policy to fit new environmental wahala and make sustainable development for the state better. The Commissioner for Environment and Na...

item.title: G8 Education to close 40 underperforming childcare centres amid falling enrolments
item.description: G8 Education has announced it will suspend operations at around one in ten of its childcare centres, citing a sharp drop in enrolments. The company attributed the decline to financial pressures on fam...

item.title: Boards Must Understand AI to Ensure Proper Governance and Accountability
item.description: Artificial intelligence is increasingly shaping decisions across South African businesses, from credit assessments in banking to product pricing in telecommunications. The concern highlighted by Ntoko...

item.title: Sindh High Court confirms KSDP-2020 remains operative until new master plan approval
item.description: The Sindh High Court (SHC) has ruled that the Karachi Strategic Development Plan (KSDP-2020) will continue to be in effect until any new master plan, including the proposed Greater Karachi Regional Pl...

item.title: Potential care method may reduce miscarriages annually, study suggests
item.description: A recent study has suggested that implementing specific healthcare measures could potentially prevent up to 10,000 miscarriages each year. Although the full article was inaccessible due to website res...

item.title: Trey Yesavage Impresses in First Start, Leads Blue Jays to 3-0 Win Over Red Sox
item.description: Trey Yesavage, the 22-year-old pitcher who made waves during last season's playoffs and World Series, made a remarkable return in his first start of the 2026 season, guiding the Toronto Blue Jays to a...

item.title: Man Forced to Carry Deceased Sister’s Remains to Bank Over Access Dispute
item.description: In a shocking and tragic event in Odisha, India, a man named Jitu Munda had to carry the skeletal remains of his late sister, Kakra Munda, to the local bank after being denied access to her savings. K...

item.title: Babak Anvari and Two & Two Pictures Partner with Range for Film Projects
item.description: Babak Anvari, the London-based Iranian filmmaker known for his BAFTA-winning debut 'Under the Shadow,' has signed with Range along with his production company, Two & Two Pictures. 'Under the Shadow,' ...

item.title: Eastern Cape Villagers Struggle to Complete Community Clinic Amid Government Delays
item.description: In Ugie, Eastern Cape, pensioners and elderly residents of eNkalweni village have been trying to build their own clinic since 2014, after repeated unsuccessful requests for government support. The com...

item.title: Dina Bonnevie returns to ABS-CBN starring in ‘Love Is Never Gone’ series
item.description: Veteran actress Dina Bonnevie is making a significant comeback to ABS-CBN through the highly anticipated drama series ‘Love Is Never Gone.’ The show marks a return for Bonnevie to the network, which i...

item.title: Former Optus CEO Kelly Bayer Rosmarin launches AI start-up
item.description: Kelly Bayer Rosmarin, former chief executive of Optus, has embarked on a new chapter as the founder of an artificial intelligence start-up. Having led one of Australia's largest telecommunications com...

item.title: Exploring the Cultural and Social Significance of Portuguese in Goa
item.description: The article discusses an excerpt from ‘Lusophone Goa: Tracing the Portuguese Language,’ highlighting the personal and cultural experiences of the author, Luis Dias, with Portuguese in Goa. Dias reflec...

item.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Applying for Asylum in France
item.description: Former Ako Bicol party-list Representative Zaldy Co is reportedly seeking political asylum in France after his release from Czech authorities. In France, asylum applications are handled by national au...

item.title: PhilHealth to provide increased support for maternity care under Marcos administration
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. announced that PhilHealth will be expanding its maternity benefits to better support pregnant women and new mothers across the Philippines. The expansion is aimed at ens...

item.title: Tamarind Brasserie at Dari Business Park closes due to ongoing property dispute
item.description: Tamarind Group has officially closed its Tamarind Brasserie restaurant at Dari Business Park along Lang'ata Road in Nairobi. The closure is a result of an ongoing legal and access dispute between Dari...
